section: Chapter 9: Mensuration Including Area of Paths

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a square?
questions.explanation: The area of a square can be calculated using the formula A = s^2, where s is the length of one side.

questions.question: What is the formula for the perimeter of a rectangle?
questions.explanation: The perimeter of a rectangle can be calculated using the formula P = 2(l + w), where l is the length and w is the width.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a triangle?
questions.explanation: The area of a triangle can be calculated using the formula A = (b * h) / 2, where b is the base and h is the height.

questions.question: What is the formula for the circumference of a circle?
questions.explanation: The circumference of a circle can be calculated using the formula C = 2πr, where r is the radius.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a parallelogram?
questions.explanation: The area of a parallelogram can be calculated using the formula A = b * h, where b is the base and h is the height.

questions.question: What is the formula for the volume of a cube?
questions.explanation: The volume of a cube can be calculated using the formula V = s^3, where s is the length of one side.

questions.question: What is the formula for the surface area of a cylinder?
questions.explanation: The surface area of a cylinder can be calculated using the formula SA = 2πr(h + r), where r is the radius and h is the height.

questions.question: What is the formula for the volume of a sphere?
questions.explanation: The volume of a sphere can be calculated using the formula V = (4/3)πr^3, where r is the radius.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of an ellipse?
questions.explanation: The area of an ellipse can be calculated using the formula A = πab, where a and b are the lengths of the semi-major and semi-minor axes.

questions.question: What is the formula for the volume of a cone?
questions.explanation: The volume of a cone can be calculated using the formula V = (1/3)πr^2h, where r is the radius and h is the height.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a sector?
questions.explanation: The area of a sector can be calculated using the formula A = (θ/360)πr^2, where θ is the central angle and r is the radius.

notes.title: Area of a Square
notes.content: The area of a square can be calculated using the formula A = s^2, where s is the length of one side.

notes.title: Perimeter of a Rectangle
notes.content: The perimeter of a rectangle can be calculated using the formula P = 2(l + w), where l is the length and w is the width.

notes.content: The area of a sector can be calculated using the formula A = (θ/360)πr^2, where θ is the central angle and r is the radius.

facts.fact: A square has four equal sides.
The perimeter of a rectangle is the sum of all its sides.
The area of a triangle can be calculated using the formula A = (b * h) / 2, where b is the base and h is the height.
A circle has no corners or edges.
The area of a parallelogram can be calculated using the formula A = b * h, where b is the base and h is the height.
A cube has six equal square faces.
The surface area of a cylinder can be calculated using the formula SA = 2πr(h + r), where r is the radius and h is the height.
A sphere has no corners or edges.
The area of an ellipse can be calculated using the formula A = πab, where a and b are the lengths of the semi-major and semi-minor axes.
A cone has a circular base.
